title: Centralize app route method enforcement
description:
Fresh-eyes quality review finding.

Evidence:
- internal/app/routes.go stores allowed methods on every appRouteDefinition, but registerAppRoutes only installs route.handler(handlers).
- Individual handlers still repeat method guards with methodNotAllowed across pro forma, exports, organization, scenarios, payments, Cost Explorer, bills, budgets, tags, resources, and other files.

Problem:
The route table looks like the source of truth for Allow headers and method support, but enforcement still lives in each handler. That makes method behavior easy to drift: a new route can update allowed without a matching guard, or a handler can change allowed methods without updating the route table tests.

Suggested outcome:
Wrap registered app routes with a shared method guard that uses appRouteDefinition.allowed. Then remove redundant per-handler guards where handlers are only reached through the app mux, or keep small direct-surface guards only where direct handler calls are intentional. Update route/method tests so the route table is the enforcement source, not just documentation.

title: Shorten scenario duration estimates
description: Update packaged scenario estimated-duration labels to the shortest plausible learner completion times, starting with First consolidated bill at 5 minutes.

title: Split app server_test.go into workflow-focused test files
description:
Fresh-eyes quality review found `internal/app/server_test.go` has grown to 9,144 lines and mixes server lifecycle, organization, resources, payments, scenarios, exports, budgets, Cost Explorer, tags, and bills coverage in one file. `rg -n "^func Test" internal/app/server_test.go` shows dozens of feature-level tests in the same package file, while nearby focused files such as `workspace_test.go` and `scenario_feedback_test.go` already exist.

Maintenance risk:
- Feature changes require navigating a very large unrelated test file.
- Shared helpers and test fixtures are harder to identify and reuse.
- Review diffs become noisy when unrelated workflow tests sit together.

Acceptance criteria:
- Move tests into workflow-focused files such as resources, bills, exports, cost_explorer, scenarios, organization, payments, budgets, and server lifecycle without changing behavior.
- Keep common helpers in clearly named shared test helper files.
- `go test ./internal/app` passes.
- No broad production-code behavior changes are included.

title: Fix stale bd list hint in br stats output
description:
Fresh-eyes workflow audit found one remaining legacy tracker hint outside the already-closed robot-next guidance: running br stats ends with a footer that points users to `bd list` for more details. That contradicts README.md, AGENTS.md, and MIND_MAP.md, which all tell agents to use br in this workspace.

## Steps to Reproduce
1. Run `br stats` from the repository root.
2. Observe the footer says to use `bd list`.
3. Compare AGENTS.md Quick Reference and Robot-Next Compatibility sections, which say to use br equivalents and not legacy tracker commands.

## Acceptance Criteria
- `br stats` no longer points agents at `bd list`, or repository guidance explicitly documents this remaining stale CLI hint until the tracker binary is fixed upstream.
- A lightweight smoke check verifies the corrected stats output or the documented exception.
- The existing `bd-haa` robot-next guidance remains intact and is not duplicated.

comments.author: David Plumpton
comments.text: Additional verification note from the same audit: immediately after creating four quality issues, `br stats` reported Total Issues: 149 and Open: 52, but its Recent Activity section still showed `Issues Created: 0`. When fixing or documenting the stats output, also verify whether recent issue creation counts are computed correctly.

title: Allow scenario definitions to rerun in the same workspace
description:
Fresh quality review found that scenario run IDs are deterministic from definition name, clock start, organization template, and random seed, while scenario_runs.id is the primary key. Running the same scenario twice in one workspace, or changing event bodies without changing those header fields, collides with the existing run audit row before execution starts. That blocks repeatable learner attempts and makes changed scenario definitions hard to audit.

## Steps to Reproduce
1. Parse and run a scenario definition in one SQLite workspace.
2. Run the same definition again in that same workspace.
3. Observe insertScenarioRun fails with a primary-key or unique constraint error on scenario_runs.id.
4. Change the event list while leaving name, clock.start, organization_template, and random_seed unchanged; observe the run ID remains the same.

## Acceptance Criteria
- A scenario can be attempted more than once in the same workspace while preserving durable audit history for each attempt.
- Stable scenario identity and per-attempt run identity are separated, or the run ID includes enough definition content and attempt data to avoid collisions.
- Tests cover rerunning the same definition in one workspace and running two definitions with the same header fields but different event bodies.

title: Avoid absolute workspace paths in review archive manifests
description:
Fresh-eyes review of the scenario archive workflow found that archiveScenarioRun writes manifest.workspace_path from h.currentWorkspacePath(). Workspace paths are normalized to absolute paths in normalizeWorkspacePath, so review archive manifests can expose a local user directory or machine-specific path when the ZIP is shared for instructor review.

Why it matters: README.md and MIND_MAP.md describe local review archive bundles as evidence artifacts. A portable evidence bundle should not need or leak the creator local filesystem path; the manifest already carries stable relative paths such as workspace/simulator.db and feedback-report.json.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Review archive manifest.json omits absolute local workspace paths, stores only a redacted/display-safe workspace label, or clearly marks the path as local-only and nonportable.
- Tests inspect manifest.json from /scenarios/archive and assert it does not expose the temp/user workspace root.
- Existing archive entries remain intact: manifest.json, feedback-report.json, workspace/simulator.db, CUR CSV, and reconciliation JSON.

title: Share Savings Plan decimal form parsing with common helpers
description:
Fresh-eyes quality review finding.

Evidence:
- internal/app/format_helpers.go has parsePositiveDecimalScaled and shared tests cover budget, threshold, resource quantity, and payment parsers.
- internal/app/savings_plan_handlers.go uses that helper for hourly commitment, but parseSavingsPlanOptionalUSDMicros still repeats normalization, ParseFloat, finite checks, negative checks, rounding, and overflow checks.
- internal/app/savings_plan_test.go exercises the happy-path form workflow but does not cover NaN/Inf/overflow/negative edge cases for Savings Plan amount parsing.

Problem:
Most decimal form parsing is centralized, but optional Savings Plan upfront fees remain a local variant. That is small today, but it is exactly the kind of cross-form validation drift this repo has been cleaning up.

Suggested outcome:
Add a shared non-negative/optional decimal-scaled helper or otherwise reuse the common parse path for Savings Plan upfront fees. Extend shared parser tests or Savings Plan tests to cover blank optional value, zero, negative, non-finite, and overflow inputs while keeping hourly commitment positive-only.

title: Centralize shared select-option and account-label helpers
description:
Fresh-eyes review finding. Evidence: the generic selectOptionsWithSelected helper lives in internal/app/cost_explorer_handlers.go:970 even though internal/app/budget_handlers.go:454 uses it too. The same shared-helper drift appears in duplicated account label/option builders: proFormaHandler.accountLabels/proFormaAccountOptions in internal/app/pro_forma_handlers.go:445 and :551 duplicate savingsPlanHandler.accountLabels/savingsPlanAccountOptions in internal/app/savings_plan_handlers.go:206 and :301. MIND_MAP.md points app-wide helpers to shared files such as ui_templates.go, display_helpers.go, or shared helper files.

Acceptance: move generic select-option helpers into a shared app helper file, centralize the repeated active member-account label/options logic, and add/adjust shared helper tests so future handlers do not copy the same boilerplate.

title: Persist selected metric totals for saved Cost Explorer report runs
description:
Fresh-eyes review found saved report run metadata still records only unblended totals.

Evidence:
- internal/persistence/migrations/0018_saved_reports.sql has last_run_total_unblended_cost_micros only.
- internal/persistence/saved_reports.go SavedReportRunUpdate only carries TotalUnblendedCostMicros.
- internal/app/cost_explorer_handlers.go handleRunCostExplorerReport records result.TotalUnblendedCostMicros regardless of selected report metric such as net_cost, blended_cost, amortized_cost, or usage_quantity.

Impact: saved report history and metadata are incomplete or misleading for reports configured around non-unblended metrics.

Acceptance:
- Persist the selected metric used for the run and the matching metric total, or persist per-metric totals explicitly.
- Add migration plus repository/handler tests covering a non-unblended saved report run.
- Keep existing unblended metadata available for compatibility.

title: Clarify and preflight scenario launches when billing period is already closed
description:
Scenario launches can fail with a low-level SQLite trigger message after earlier actions have already created resources/usage in the workspace.

Observed in tmp/workspace after launching scenarios in a mixed order:
- First consolidated bill succeeded and closed payer 999988887777 for 2026-03-01 to 2026-04-01.
- Later launches of Payment Failure, Missing Tags, and First consolidated bill failed during run_daily_metering.
- User-facing error example: launch scenario: insert bill line item for metering record "mtr_5a64bc798f1fcbf3": constraint failed: billing period is closed for payer (1811).
- Database integrity and foreign keys are fine; the invariant is intentional: closed payer periods must not accept new bill line items.
- The confusing part is the raw trigger error and partial scenario setup before the failure. Failed runs left resources/usage/metering rows behind, and unpriced metering backlog can cause repeated failures.

Expected behavior:
- Keep the closed-period protection intact.
- Before launching an embedded scenario, preflight its billing periods/payer against existing closed periods when the scenario is known to create or price usage for those periods.
- If preflight cannot catch every case, translate the repository/trigger failure into a clear app-level message.

Suggested message:
Cannot price March 2026 usage because billing period 2026-03-01 to 2026-04-01 is already closed for payer 999988887777. Reset or clone the workspace before launching this scenario.

Acceptance criteria:
- Launching a packaged scenario that would price usage into an already closed payer period fails before creating new scenario resources/usage when this can be determined from the scenario definition.
- Any remaining closed-period pricing failure is surfaced as a clear learner-facing error naming the payer account and billing period, not SQLite constraint code 1811.
- Scenario audit/progress rows still record failed attempts honestly.
- Existing behavior preventing mutation of finalized bills, final line items, Cost Category assignments, and split allocations remains covered by tests.
- Add a regression test that closes March for AnyCompany, attempts a conflicting packaged scenario launch, and verifies the UI/error text and lack of partial setup for the preflighted path.

title: Clarify invoice PDF placeholder status in public docs
description:
Fresh-eyes cohesion finding. README.md line 42 says printable invoice pages and invoice line-item CSV export are current features, while MIND_MAP.md node [20] correctly notes that the PDF route currently returns an implementation plan rather than a generated PDF artifact. The UI exposes PDF actions and `bd-29f.2.4` tracks the deferred renderer, so the public README can leave a reader expecting PDF output that is not actually implemented.

Acceptance criteria:
- Update README.md or the nearest public-contract docs to distinguish printable HTML invoices and CSV export from the reserved `/invoices/{invoice_id}/document.pdf` placeholder.
- Keep MIND_MAP.md node [20] and open/deferred issue `bd-29f.2.4` aligned with the wording.
- Avoid implying tax-valid or production invoice artifacts are generated.
- Use a docs-only verification pass such as checking the README/MIND_MAP mentions for invoice, CSV, and PDF.

title: Show restricted invoice state for member bill viewers
description: Verified quality issue: member-account Bills views are correctly scoped to the member's usage rows, and invoice document routes return 403 for member viewers. For issued bills, the member-scoped bill summary suppresses invoice IDs as intended, but the UI currently labels the invoice cell as "not issued" and drops scoped CUR export/reconciliation actions. Keep member bill summaries informational by rendering a restricted invoice state for viewers whose policy cannot view invoices while preserving member-safe CUR export/reconciliation links.

title: Extract scenario runner preflight and audit orchestration
description:
Fresh-eyes review: `internal/scenario/runner.go` is still 1,891 lines after the action-spec refactor. `Runner.Run` owns validation, run audit creation, learner-progress start/complete, closed-period preflight, organization reset, clock setup, event loop aggregation, failure handling, and progress event persistence around lines 66-149. The same file also owns execution state, closed-period conflict types, preflight planning and DB conflict lookup helpers around lines 180-620, plus many action support helpers later.

Maintenance risk:
- Scenario launch/audit changes and closed-period pricing checks require editing the same file as action execution support code.
- Preflight conflicts use a separate simulated execution state, making it easy for preflight and apply paths to drift.
- Failure handling, audit persistence, and learner progress updates are repeated orchestration concerns hidden inside the main runner.

Acceptance criteria:
- Extract closed-period pricing preflight/conflict lookup into a focused runner preflight unit.
- Extract run audit/progress orchestration helpers so `Runner.Run` reads as high-level flow rather than persistence detail.
- Preserve existing learner-facing error text and packaged scenario behavior; run scenario parser/runner tests.

title: Use one archive timestamp for scenario review bundle metadata and filename
description: Fresh-eyes review found archiveScenarioRunWithSnapshot in internal/app/scenario_workspace_actions.go records archivedAt with one time.Now() call, then builds the ZIP filename with a second time.Now() call. If the calls cross a second boundary, manifest archived_at, CUR export generated_at, and the archive filename disagree. This is low-risk but confusing for review bundles that are meant to be deterministic evidence packages. Acceptance: capture the archive time once, derive archived_at, export GeneratedAt, and filename timestamp from that value, and add/update archive tests to assert the manifest timestamp and filename timestamp are consistent.

title: Add release version number to build info
description: Add an application release version to the Go binary build info so packaged builds can report a human-readable version. Acceptance criteria: define a single version source for release builds, embed it through Go build metadata or ldflags, expose it through a command or existing runtime diagnostics, and add focused tests/docs for the expected default development value and release override.

title: Add fresh workspace start action
description: Add a first-class fresh-start action that creates a clean local workspace directory, opens it, persists it as last-used state, and exposes it from the workspace and overview pages.

title: Centralize simulated viewer policy resolution
description:
Fresh-eyes quality review found repeated simulated viewer parsing and defaulting across billing workflows:
- `billsHandler.billingPolicyFromFilter` parses `viewer_role`/`viewer_account_id`, defaults management and finance account IDs, and has an unscoped mode.
- `exportsHandler.exportPolicyFromValues` and `paymentsHandler.paymentPolicyFromValues` parse the same fields but default a blank role to management.
- `costExplorerHandler.costExplorerVisibilityFilter` resolves owner role/account fields with similar management-account defaulting.
- Role select construction is duplicated as `billsViewerRoleSelect` and `exportsViewerRoleSelect`, and payments reuse the exports select helper.

Maintenance risk:
- Small visibility changes must be repeated across bills, exports, payments, and Cost Explorer.
- Default-scope differences are easy to miss and have historically produced quality follow-ups in this repo.
- Reusing exports-owned helpers from payments/bills makes ownership unclear.

Acceptance criteria:
- Introduce one shared viewer-scope/policy resolver with explicit options for unscoped/default-management behavior and required permission.
- Consolidate viewer role select/view-field construction.
- Add focused tests for bills, exports, payments, and Cost Explorer viewer defaults and denial cases.
- Existing visibility behavior remains unchanged unless tests document an intentional correction.

title: Scope Cost Explorer queries by viewer policy
description: Cost Explorer report, CSV, and drilldown paths carry owner_role and owner_account_id but do not apply billingvisibility row scope. A member-account owner can request unfiltered consolidated spend and source line items. Success: member Cost Explorer views, CSV exports, saved report runs, and drilldowns are constrained to the member usage account while management, finance, and instructor retain their documented scopes.

title: Scope saved reports by owner role everywhere
description: Saved-report shelves are treated as owner_account_id + owner_role in the Cost Explorer UI and List/GetForOwner paths, but persistence and scenario helpers still key some behavior only by owner_account_id. Evidence: internal/persistence/migrations/0018_saved_reports.sql defines idx_saved_reports_owner_name on (owner_account_id, lower(name)) without owner_role; internal/persistence/saved_reports.go GetByName filters owner_account_id + lower(name) only; internal/scenario/runner.go existingScenarioSavedReport also rewrites by owner_account_id + lower(name); internal/scenario/checks.go saved_report_exists likewise ignores owner_role. This prevents a finance and management shelf for the same payer from using the same report name and can cause scenario checks/reruns to match or rewrite the wrong role-specific report. Acceptance: include owner_role in the uniqueness/indexing contract or otherwise enforce the intended shelf model consistently; update repository GetByName, scenario rerun detection, and saved_report_exists checks/tests so same payer + same report name under different roles behaves deterministically.

title: Reject encoded path separators in download route parsers
description: Fresh-eyes review found exportFileDownloadFilenameFromPath in internal/app/export_paths.go and invoiceRouteFromPath in internal/app/bill_downloads.go check the raw URL path for / before PathUnescape, then return the unescaped value without re-checking separators. Requests such as /exports/files/a%2Fb.csv or /invoices/SIM%2FINV can therefore parse as a single filename/invoice ID containing /. Normal generated metadata is sanitized, but the route helpers should enforce one-segment semantics themselves. Acceptance: add tests for encoded / and encoded backslash on export and invoice paths, keep escaped spaces working, and return false/404 for decoded path separators.

title: Add overview intro landing page for simulator workflows
description:
Create a learner-facing overview/intro web page that explains what the AWS Billing Simulator offers and how to begin using it safely.

Motivation:
New learners can currently jump into feature areas, but there is no single guided page that describes the simulator mental model, what workflows are available, how features interact, or how to reset/start fresh without damaging a useful workspace.

Scope:
- Add an overview/intro page reachable from the app navigation and suitable as a first stop after opening a workspace.
- Describe the available product areas: Organizations, Resources/Usage, billing pipeline, Bills/Invoices/Payments, Scenarios, Tags, Cost Categories/split charges, Cost Explorer, Budgets, Exports, and Query Lab.
- Explain the core interaction flow: organization/accounts create visibility context; resources produce usage; metering/pricing creates bill line items; closes issue bills/invoices; payments modify invoice state; tags and Cost Categories affect reporting/allocation; exports/query lab consume generated billing data; scenarios seed repeatable labs.
- Explain safe starting paths: open or create a workspace, launch a packaged scenario, reset a scenario to seed, clone a workspace for experimentation, and start from a fresh workspace.
- Include clear local/synthetic safety boundaries: no AWS credentials, no real payments, no tax-valid invoices, deterministic synthetic pricing.
- Link from each overview section to the relevant app area.

Acceptance criteria:
- The page renders in a fresh workspace and without requiring any scenario to be launched first.
- Navigation exposes the page clearly without hiding existing workflows.
- Copy is concise but complete enough for a first-time learner to understand what is available and what to do next.
- Reset/start-fresh guidance distinguishes scenario reset, workspace clone, and creating a new workspace.
- Existing feature pages remain reachable and unchanged except for any intentional navigation addition.
- Add focused server/UI tests or smoke coverage that verifies the page route, key explanatory text, and major workflow links.

title: Document agent-safe local state path for README run command
description:
Fresh-eyes doc audit found that README.md Running Locally uses an explicit project workspace path but does not pass -state. The CLI default -state path is the per-user app config file, and workspaceSession.Open saves the last workspace path there even when -workspace is supplied. Agents following README.md can write outside the project while AGENTS.md says never touch files outside the project directory.

## Steps to Reproduce
1. Read README.md lines 81-85.
2. Run `go run ./cmd/billing-sim -help` and note the default `-state` path under the per-user config directory.
3. Read `internal/app/workspace.go`, where opening an explicit workspace calls `workspaceStateStore.Save`.
4. Compare AGENTS.md line 49, which says agents must never touch files outside the project directory.

## Acceptance Criteria
- README.md either includes `-state ./tmp/state.json` in the local smoke/run command or has a clearly separated agent-safe command using only project-local paths.
- AGENTS.md or MIND_MAP.md records the expected state-path convention if agents should always override the CLI default.
- Verification documents that following the agent-safe command does not write app state outside the repository.

title: Create project README
description:
Document the AWS Billing Simulator project for new contributors and users.

## Acceptance Criteria
- README explains why the simulator exists and who it is for.

title: Refactor scenario DSL event handling into typed action specs
description:
Fresh-eyes quality review found the scenario DSL action model is now a wide shared struct plus multiple action switches:
- `internal/scenario/parser.go` defines one `Event` struct with fields for accounts, resources, usage, Cost Categories, split charges, payments, budgets, and saved reports.
- Event normalization is a long field-by-field trim block in `normalizeEvent`.
- Validation is dispatched through a large switch in `validateEvent`.
- Execution is dispatched through another large switch in `Runner.applyEvent` in `internal/scenario/runner.go`.

Maintenance risk:
- Adding a scenario action requires touching several long, weakly coupled blocks.
- Action-specific required fields are not visible from the type shape.
- Unrelated action fields can accidentally interact because every action shares the same struct.

Acceptance criteria:
- Introduce action-specific specs, request structs, or an action registry that owns parse/normalize/validate/execute behavior per action.
- Preserve JSON/YAML compatibility for existing packaged scenarios.
- Keep learner-facing validation messages at least as specific as today.
- Existing scenario parser, runner, and packaged scenario tests pass.

title: Make repository timestamp defaults explicit and deterministic
description:
Fresh-eyes review finding. Evidence: normalizedRepositoryTimestamp is defined in internal/persistence/cost_allocation_tags.go:994 and falls back to time.Now().UTC() when callers pass a blank timestamp. The helper is used by cost allocation tags, saved report run metadata, and scenario learner progress in internal/persistence/cost_allocation_tags.go, internal/persistence/saved_reports.go, and internal/persistence/scenario_learner_progress.go. Current UI/scenario paths usually pass simulator-clock times, but the package-level fallback makes deterministic behavior depend on every caller remembering to do so.

Acceptance: make timestamp defaults explicit by requiring timestamps for deterministic repositories or routing defaults through an injectable/simulator clock boundary. Add tests covering blank timestamp behavior for the affected repository methods.

title: Migrate another scenario action family to typed payload adapter
description: Follow-up to bd-kb1w. Payment lifecycle events now use scenarioEventPayloadActionSpec. Resource and usage, billing-period, Cost Category, budget, Savings Plan, and saved-report actions still normalize, validate, and apply against the broad Event. Pick one cohesive action family, introduce a narrow payload behind the existing JSON/YAML fields, preserve validation text, and add parser/action coverage.

title: Split pro forma billing repository by responsibility
description:
Fresh-eyes quality review finding.

Evidence:
- internal/persistence/pro_forma_billing.go is about 1,600 lines and currently contains public repository methods, pricing-plan/rule/group/custom-line-item CRUD, generated line-item refresh, source-line queries, summary queries, request normalization/validation, status/type helpers, and row scanners.
- internal/app/pro_forma_handlers.go is also large, but the persistence file has the heavier domain mix and is the clearer first simplification target.

Problem:
The repository file now owns several separate responsibilities. That makes pro forma changes harder to review because a small generated-row fix sits next to CRUD, validation, summaries, and scanners. It also increases merge risk as pro forma labs grow.

Suggested outcome:
Split pro forma persistence into focused files such as models/types, CRUD repository methods, generation/refresh, summaries/listing, validation/normalization, and scanners. Keep package-private names where possible and avoid behavior changes. Verify with existing pro forma persistence/app tests and add focused tests only if the split reveals missing coverage.

title: Neutralize spreadsheet formulas in downloadable CSV exports
description:
Fresh-eyes quality finding. CSV syntax is handled with encoding/csv, but exported string cells are not neutralized for spreadsheet formula interpretation. User-controlled or scenario-controlled values can flow into invoice CSV, Cost Explorer result CSV, CUR-like CSV, and FOCUS-like CSV records; examples include resource names/IDs, account names, tags_json, cost_categories_json, descriptions, group values, and service/account labels. A value beginning with =, +, -, or @ can be interpreted by Excel/Sheets when a learner opens the downloaded CSV.

Evidence:
- internal/app/bill_downloads.go invoiceCSVRecord writes item.ResourceName and item.Description directly.
- internal/app/cost_explorer_exports.go costExplorerResultsCSVRecord writes group values directly.
- internal/persistence/cur_exports.go curCSVExportRecord writes account_name, resource_id, tags_json, cost_categories_json, and description directly.
- internal/persistence/focus_exports.go focusCSVExportRecord writes account/resource/tag/category strings directly.

Acceptance criteria:
- Define a consistent CSV cell safety policy for downloadable files without breaking Query Lab data-fidelity expectations.
- Add a shared helper or explicit export-mode handling for spreadsheet-dangerous leading characters in user-controlled string cells.
- Cover invoice CSV, Cost Explorer CSV, CUR-like CSV, and FOCUS-like CSV with tests using values that start with =, +, -, and @.
- Keep numeric columns machine-readable, and document any deliberate exception where raw data fidelity wins over spreadsheet safety.

title: Move persisted CUR export generation behind explicit action
description: Fresh-eyes review finding. GET /exports/cur.csv writes workspace export files and upserts workspace_export_files metadata through writeCURCSVExportFile when workspacePath is set. That means a download, reload, or crawler can mutate workspace state, unlike tag discovery and budget refresh flows that use explicit POST actions for persisted changes. Acceptance criteria: decide the intended generation/download contract, add an explicit POST or generate action for persisted exports, make GET and HEAD downloads read-only or clearly separate direct CSV responses from stored export creation, and cover no-mutation-on-GET plus persisted generation behavior.

title: Replace brittle string-matched validation tests with typed errors
description:
Fresh-eyes review finding. Evidence: many validation/error tests assert strings.Contains(err.Error(), ...) across persistence and app packages, including internal/persistence/payment_lifecycle_test.go:247, internal/persistence/budgets_test.go:480, internal/persistence/cost_categories_test.go:554, internal/persistence/resource_usage_test.go:320, and internal/app/shared_helpers_test.go:400. Some domains already have typed sentinels such as ErrSavedReportNotFound and ErrClosedBillingPeriod, but many high-value validation paths still require preserving incidental wording.

Acceptance: pick one high-churn domain first, introduce typed sentinel/domain errors for the important branches, switch tests to errors.Is/errors.As for semantics, and keep separate message assertions only where learner-facing copy is the contract.

title: Preserve viewer scope on invoice page actions
description: Fresh-eyes quality finding. After scoped Bills links open an invoice with viewer_role/viewer_account_id, the invoice page action bar renders CSV, PDF, and Bills links without those viewer fields. The PDF placeholder response also points its alternate printable HTML link at the unscoped invoice URL. This drops the selected simulated billing persona mid-workflow and can bypass route-level visibility checks if the generated links are reused without the original query. Acceptance: scoped invoice pages preserve viewer fields on CSV/PDF/Bills actions, the PDF placeholder Link header and body preserve the same viewer fields, unscoped invoice pages remain unchanged, and focused server coverage proves the scoped links.

title: Restrict health check route to safe HTTP methods
description: Both mux definitions register /healthz with a handler that ignores the request method, so POST, PUT, and other unsupported methods receive 200 ok. Other read-only routes explicitly allow only GET and HEAD. Acceptance: /healthz accepts GET and HEAD, unsupported methods return 405 with an Allow header, and tests cover both workspace and direct mux startup paths if needed.

title: Make scenario review archives use a consistent SQLite snapshot
description:
Fresh-eyes quality finding. Scenario review archives checkpoint the workspace database and then copy workspace/simulator.db into the ZIP, but the archive request only holds the normal shared request lease. Other DB-backed requests can run concurrently under the same RLock while the archive is building. That means a POST or partial-refresh-triggered write can happen after PRAGMA wal_checkpoint(TRUNCATE) and before/during os.ReadFile of simulator.db, leaving the archived database stale or, in the worst case, copied during a write.

Evidence:
- internal/app/workspace_handlers.go workspaceRouteUsesActiveDB gives DB routes shared read leases; /scenarios/archive is not excluded, so it does not take the exclusive workspace swap lock.
- internal/app/scenario_workspace_actions.go archiveScenarioRun calls checkpointWorkspaceDB and then addFileToZip on persistence.WorkspaceDBPath(workspacePath).
- Clone/reset paths take workspaceSession.swapMu.Lock before checkpoint/copy or replacement; archive does not.

Impact:
Review ZIPs are supposed to be evidence bundles containing the workspace database plus CUR/reconciliation evidence. A concurrent write can make the bundled simulator.db not match the manifest/exports or miss latest learner state.

Acceptance criteria:
- Build archives from a transactionally consistent SQLite snapshot, either by using SQLite backup APIs or by serializing archive database copying against all DB writes with an exclusive workspace/session lock.
- Add a regression test that holds or performs a concurrent DB write around archive creation and proves the archived simulator.db is complete and readable.
- Keep reset/clone deadlock protections intact; /scenarios/archive should not block itself through the request-lease middleware.
- Document the snapshot timing in code comments or test names so future archive evidence changes preserve it.

title: Return Allow headers for 405 responses
description: methodNotAllowed in internal/app/resource_handlers.go returns 405 with only Content-Type and no Allow header, and every guarded handler calls that helper. HTTP 405 responses should advertise the permitted methods for the route. Acceptance: methodNotAllowed or its call sites can express allowed methods, tests cover GET/HEAD and POST-only routes, and unsupported methods return an Allow header such as GET, HEAD or POST.

title: Avoid silent truncation in bill and Cost Explorer detail summaries
description:
Fresh-eyes review finding.

Evidence:
- internal/app/bill_handlers.go:536 loads ListBillStateSummaries with Limit: 50, then internal/app/bill_handlers.go:544 computes billStateCards from only that returned slice. A workspace with more than the capped summaries can show understated bill-state counts and totals with no capped-results warning.
- internal/app/cost_explorer_handlers.go:529-546 loads Cost Explorer drilldown source line items with Limit: 100, then internal/app/cost_explorer_handlers.go:553-556 labels the state cards from len(items), summed cost, and summed usage. Aggregate rows with more than 100 source items will show partial totals as if they were complete.
- Bills charge/reconciliation sections also use fixed display limits (50/75) without exposing whether additional rows exist.

Impact:
Learners can get incorrect-looking financial counts/totals from UI summaries once a workspace has enough periods, payers, services, resources, or line items. This is especially risky for reconciliation and Cost Explorer teaching because the UI does not distinguish complete totals from a capped subset.

Acceptance notes:
- Compute headline counts/totals with uncapped aggregate queries, or explicitly label capped sections as showing the first N rows and keep state cards tied to complete totals.
- For Cost Explorer drilldowns, return total matching line-item count/cost/usage separately from the displayed page of line items.
- Add tests with more rows than the display limits that prove the summary cards either remain complete or visibly indicate truncation.

title: Unify app route registration to prevent workspace mux drift
description:
Fresh-eyes quality finding. Route registration is duplicated between internal/app/server.go newMux and internal/app/workspace_handlers.go newWorkspaceMux. The production server uses newWorkspaceMux, but tests and helper code can use newMux; the two registries must be manually kept in sync across dozens of routes. The split already creates a misleading direct-mux surface: shared page nav and empty states render /workspaces links from layout.go/ui_templates.go, while newMux does not register /workspaces, /workspaces/open, or /workspaces/start.

Impact:
- Smoke tests against newMux can pass while production workspace routing differs, or fail on links that production supports.
- Future route additions can land in one mux and silently miss the other.
- Method-contract tests can cover the wrong handler graph.

Evidence:
- internal/app/server.go has a standalone newMux route list.
- internal/app/workspace_handlers.go has a second large newWorkspaceMux route list with workspace-aware wrappers.
- internal/app/layout.go always includes a Workspaces nav link.

Acceptance criteria:
- Extract a shared route registration function or adapter so the runtime workspace mux and direct DB/test mux cannot drift.
- Keep workspace-only routes explicit, but make the distinction obvious and tested.
- Add a route parity test that proves every DB-backed app route is registered in both intended surfaces with the expected method behavior.
- Ensure links rendered by direct-mux pages either resolve or are intentionally absent in that surface.

title: Polish AGENTS version-control wording and ASCII drift
description:

The quick-reference commands are correct, but the typo and glyph drift reduce clarity for future agents.

## Acceptance Criteria
- AGENTS.md spells Jujutsu correctly in the version-control bullet.
- The status-transition wording is either ASCII-only or the project explicitly accepts the arrow as an intentional docs exception.
- A simple scan such as `rg -n "[^[:ascii:]]" AGENTS.md` has documented expected output after the change.

title: Close workspace database on unexpected server exit
description:
Fresh quality review found app.Run returns directly from the server.done branch when http.Server.Serve exits before context cancellation. If Start opened a workspace database, that path does not call Server.Close, so the database handle can remain open after an unexpected serve failure.

## Steps to Reproduce
1. Start the app with a workspace path so Start opens a SQLite database.
2. Force the serving goroutine to exit before the Run context is cancelled, for example by closing the listener or simulating a Serve error.
3. Observe Run returns from the server.done branch without calling Server.Close.
4. The workspace database cleanup path is skipped on that unexpected serve exit.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Run closes the workspace database and listener cleanup path on both cancellation and serve-error exits.
- A focused lifecycle test covers the unexpected serve-exit branch.
- The normal graceful-shutdown path still returns the Serve result through Wait without double-closing the database.

title: Add seed/catalog parity tests for packaged scenarios
description:
Fresh-eyes test review found that `scenarioCatalog()` in `internal/app/scenario_handlers.go` silently falls back to `defaultScenarioMetadata()` when a packaged seed has no curated UI metadata. Current `TestScenarioCatalogUsesShortestPlausibleDurations` in `internal/app/scenarios_test.go` checks durations for known keys, but it does not assert exact parity with `scenario.SeedDefinitionKeys()` or that every embedded seed has non-default phase/objective/resume metadata.

Add a focused test that loads all packaged seed keys and fails if `scenarioCatalog()` has missing or extra keys, blank curated fields, default fallback values, or non-launchable resume paths. This keeps new scenario labs from shipping with generic catalog copy or missing metadata.

title: Move cost allocation tag discovery refresh off GET /tags
description:
Fresh review found internal/app/tag_handlers.go loadTagsPageData calls CostAllocationTagRepository.RefreshDiscoveredTags while serving GET /tags. That repository inserts/updates cost_allocation_tag_keys, deletes stale inventory, and upserts inventory rows. The page also exposes a 'Refresh Discovery' anchor to /tags, so ordinary page loads and link prefetch/refresh behavior can mutate discovery state.

## Acceptance Criteria
- GET /tags renders persisted discovery, inventory, coverage, and activation data without writing cost allocation tag tables.
- Tag discovery refresh moves behind an explicit POST/action or another clearly named simulator workflow.
- App coverage proves repeated GET /tags is database-idempotent, and separate coverage proves the explicit refresh action still discovers new resource tag keys/values.

title: Recover workspace session if scenario reset fails mid-swap
description:
Evidence: internal/app/scenario_workspace_actions.go:256 clears s.db and s.path before closing the old DB and deleting/reopening simulator.db. If removeWorkspaceDatabaseFiles, persistence.OpenWorkspace, or s.store.Save fails afterward, ResetToScenarioSeed returns with the session detached from the previous workspace; if Save fails after opening the reset DB, the new DB also remains active without persisted state cleanup.

Impact: a failed reset can leave the browser session with no active workspace or partially reset state, forcing the learner to reopen manually and making the error harder to recover from.

Acceptance: make reset failure behavior transactional at the session level: either restore/keep a usable workspace handle when cleanup/open/save fails, or deliberately reopen the reset workspace and report a recoverable error. Add focused app/workspace tests for failures after s.db is cleared, including at least one failure before reopening and one after opening the replacement DB.

title: Keep MIND_MAP session notes compact
description:
Fresh-eyes review of README.md, AGENTS.md, and MIND_MAP.md found that the mind-map maintenance rules and AGENTS.md both ask for concise, actionable session notes, but MIND_MAP.md nodes [14], [15], and [29] have drifted back toward long per-ticket diaries. Node [14] is over 6000 characters and nodes [15]/[29] repeat many specific issue IDs, which makes the file harder to use as the compact knowledge index described at the top of MIND_MAP.md.

Why it matters: agents are instructed to start from this file every session. Overlong diary nodes hide the reusable rules, make line-based edits risky, and reintroduce the maintenance problem that bd-2mx was meant to reduce.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Nodes [14], [15], and [29] are condensed into durable patterns, current preferences, and only the most useful recent examples.
- The update preserves protected nodes [1]-[5] and keeps node references valid.
- A lightweight text check documents that the largest non-format nodes are materially shorter and the file remains navigable.
close_reason: Condensed MIND_MAP diary nodes and documented text-check evidence.

comments.author: David Plumpton
comments.text: Text check: node [14] condensed 6401 -> 1277 chars, [15] 1559 -> 740, [29] 2617 -> 897. Largest non-format node is now [21] at 2509 chars, protected nodes [1]-[5] were untouched, and a reference scan reported all references resolve.

title: Record Cost Explorer UI saved-report runs
description:
Fresh bd-1of.2 close-out smoke found saved reports still show Last Run as never_run after a saved report is loaded and rendered through /cost-explorer. The repository supports last-run metadata, but the browser report execution path is currently GET-only and does not call SavedReportRepository.RecordLastRun.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Running a saved Cost Explorer report through the UI records successful last-run status, row count, total unblended cost, and timestamp without relying on unsafe GET side effects.
- The Saved Reports table shows the updated last-run status/time after that UI run.
- A focused app test covers create saved report -> run saved report -> persisted/displayed last-run metadata.

title: Consolidate MIND_MAP.md
description: Condense the project mind map into a compact navigable index while preserving protected format nodes and current project/workflow context.

title: Evaluate all packaged scenario checks in tests
description:
Fresh-eyes test review found that `TestEvaluatorEvaluatesPackagedScenarioChecks` in `internal/scenario/checks_test.go` hard-codes only `ForecastBudgetAlertSeedKey` and `SharedNetworkingAllocationSeedKey`. Those are the only current packaged seeds with `checks`, but the test will not automatically cover future seed files that add assessment checks.

Change the test to derive its cases from `SeedDefinitionKeys()` and include every packaged seed whose parsed definition has one or more checks. It should still assert all authored checks pass after running the seed, and should make failures name the seed key and check IDs. This prevents authored scenario checks from drifting silently when new checked labs are added.

title: Extract payment action dispatch from payment page rendering
description:
Fresh-eyes review: `internal/app/payment_handlers.go` is 989 lines and mixes page rendering, data loading, authorization, payment action dispatch, view rows, status gating, amount parsing, and the raw Payments template. The `applyPaymentAction` switch at line 281 handles invoice lifecycle transitions, profile/default-method changes, method repair, repository calls, and flash-copy generation, while the same file renders due invoices/history/setup and defines template markup around line 771.

Maintenance risk:
- Adding a payment action requires touching a large page handler and duplicating parse/authorize/apply/flash conventions.
- Invoice lifecycle actions and payment-profile setup actions have different domain rules but share one switch.
- UI rendering changes and mutation workflow changes are interleaved, making tests harder to target.

Acceptance criteria:
- Extract payment actions into a command/action registry or workflow layer that owns parse, visibility check, repository call, and flash message per action.
- Keep page data loading/view rows separate from state-changing payment commands.
- Preserve existing routes/forms and viewer-scope enforcement; run `go test ./internal/app`.

title: Scope Cost Explorer saved reports by viewer or owner
description:
Fresh review found the browser Cost Explorer page calls SavedReportRepository.List with only Limit:100 in internal/app/cost_explorer_handlers.go, then renders every saved report owner in the Saved Reports table. The persistence layer and billingvisibility policy model support owner role/account scoping, and tests cover repository filtering only when OwnerAccountID and OwnerRole are supplied. The UI therefore bypasses that scoping and can list/load reports for unrelated simulated personas.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Cost Explorer UI derives a viewer/owner context and passes OwnerAccountID plus OwnerRole when listing saved reports.
- Loading/updating a saved report enforces the same owner/visibility scope rather than accepting any saved_report_id visible in the workspace.
- Focused app test creates reports for management and a member account, then verifies each viewer sees and loads only the expected reports.

title: Preserve viewer scope in bills invoice links
description: Fresh-eyes quality finding. The Bills page preserved simulated viewer scope on CUR export/reconciliation links, but invoice HTML/CSV/PDF links from scoped Bills views were rendered without viewer_role/viewer_account_id. Invoice routes also checked only whether the selected role could view invoices, not whether the selected payer-scoped viewer could view the invoice payer. Acceptance: invoice links generated from scoped Bills views include the same viewer fields, member invoice/CSV/PDF clicks remain forbidden, cross-payer management invoice/CSV/PDF clicks are forbidden, matching management/finance links continue to work, and focused server coverage proves the scoped links and route enforcement.

title: Make MIND_MAP backlog status a dated snapshot or live-command pointer

- Node [12] makes clear that ready-work recommendations are historical/session context and that live `br ready` or label-scoped `br ready --label <label>` is authoritative.
- The update stays compact and preserves the mind-map graph structure with no missing node references.

title: Snapshot resource tags by usage window, not recording time
description:
Fresh quality review found RecordUsageEvent snapshots whatever resource tags are active at the time the event is recorded. Because activeTags ignores resource_tags.applied_at and removed_at, backfilled usage for a window before a tag was applied can be stored and billed as tagged. This undermines missing/late tag lessons and makes historical billing attribution misleading.

## Steps to Reproduce
1. Create a resource that has no owner tag at 2026-02-01T00:00:00Z.
2. Add owner=platform after that usage window.
3. Record usage for 2026-02-01T00:00:00Z through 2026-02-01T01:00:00Z.
4. Observe usage_events.tag_snapshot_json includes the current owner tag even though the tag was not active during the usage window.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Usage event tag snapshots are selected according to the usage window, not wall-clock recording time.
- Tests cover tags applied after usage began and tags removed before usage.
- Future Cost Explorer and tag activation work can distinguish untagged historical usage from currently tagged resources.

title: Allow partially paid past-due invoices to be marked due
description: The documented payment workflow says a partial collection against a past-due invoice should keep the bill past_due while the invoice is partially_paid until the remaining balance succeeds or is explicitly marked due. Today PaymentLifecycleRepository.validateInvoicePaymentTransition does not allow partially_paid -> due, and the Payments UI hides the Mark Due action for partially_paid invoices, so learners cannot return a partially paid past-due invoice to ordinary due state without retrying collection. Add regression coverage for the repository and UI path, allow the transition, and show the Mark Due action when a partially paid invoice still has an amount due.

title: Split Resource Lab resource editing from billing operations
description:
Fresh-eyes review: `internal/app/resource_handlers.go` is still a mixed resource inventory and billing-control surface. `newResourceLabHandler` wires resources, catalog, metering, line items, clock, daily jobs, and month-end repositories at line 256; handlers from about lines 269-704 cover resource CRUD/tagging/usage, billing pipeline runs, daily metering, month-end close, clock advance, data loading, and rendering; the raw page template begins at line 706 and includes all of those workflows.

Maintenance risk:
- Resource inventory changes and billing-period operation changes require touching the same handler/template.
- `loadResourcePageData` assembles resources, usage events, metering records, bill line items, summaries, daily jobs, closes, issued bills, and price catalog rows in one page loader.
- The Resources page can keep absorbing billing-control responsibilities because there is no obvious split.

Acceptance criteria:
- Extract billing/clock/month-end actions and view assembly into named workflow file(s) or components while preserving existing routes and behavior.
- Keep resource create/tag/usage form parsing and inventory views in resource-focused code.
- Add or adjust focused app tests if behavior moves; `go test ./internal/app` passes.

title: Extract remaining large app templates from handler files
description:
Fresh-eyes review finding. Evidence: internal/app/scenario_handlers.go:695 and internal/app/scenario_handlers.go:806 still embed large page templates in the handler file; internal/app/pro_forma_handlers.go:653 and internal/app/cost_category_handlers.go:649 do the same. Several newer areas already use dedicated template files such as internal/app/payment_templates.go, internal/app/resource_templates.go, internal/app/export_templates.go, and internal/app/savings_plan_templates.go.

Acceptance: move the high-churn remaining page templates out of handler files into feature-specific *_templates.go files, keep behavior unchanged, and run the focused app tests for the moved surfaces.

title: Let Query Lab examples use a selected generated export path
description:
Evidence: internal/app/query_lab_handlers.go:9 defines queryLabCSVPathPlaceholder as /path/to/export.csv, and handleQueryLab always renders CSVPath and SQL examples from that constant at lines 38-42. The page tells learners to replace the path manually, while /exports rows expose download/reconciliation actions but no Query Lab action with the generated workspace-local file path.

Impact: the query-lab workflow is less local-first than the rest of the simulator. After generating a CUR CSV, learners still have to discover and manually substitute the file path into every SQL example.

Acceptance: allow /query-lab to accept a selected CSV path or export filename, add a Query Lab action for generated CUR CSV files in the Exports table, and render SQL examples with the selected path. Preserve the external-DuckDB/no-embedded-runtime decision and add focused handler/view tests.

title: Extract app-wide helpers from feature handler files
description:
Fresh-eyes quality review found shared app helpers living in unrelated feature files. Examples:
- `methodNotAllowed`, `formatUSDMicros`, `formatQuantityMicros`, `keyValueViews`, `flashFromQuery`, and `urlQueryEscape` are defined in `internal/app/resource_handlers.go` but used across many app pages.
- `exportViewerFields`, `exportsViewerRoleSelect`, `appendQueryValue`, and viewer path helpers are defined in `internal/app/export_handlers.go` but are used by bills and payments.
- `displayOptionalValue` lives in `bill_handlers.go` and `titleLabel` lives in `organization_handlers.go` while both are general display helpers.

Maintenance risk:
- Unrelated files depend on feature handler implementation details.
- New workflows are encouraged to add more generic code to arbitrary large handlers.
- Helper ownership is unclear during refactors.

Acceptance criteria:
- Move shared HTTP, formatting, query, viewer-field, and display helpers into clearly named shared app files.
- Feature handler files no longer own helpers used broadly outside their workflow.
- Keep existing helper behavior covered by focused tests.
- `go test ./internal/app` passes.

title: Reject non-finite decimal form inputs before micros conversion
description:
Evidence: parseBudgetAmountMicros and parseBudgetThresholdBasisPoints in internal/app/budget_handlers.go:478 and :497, parseQuantityMicros in internal/app/resource_forms.go:323, and parsePaymentAmountMicros in internal/app/payment_actions.go:296 all use strconv.ParseFloat followed by math.Round and integer conversion. They check ordinary parse errors, <= 0, and large finite values, but do not explicitly reject math.IsNaN before rounding/converting.

Impact: values like NaN can bypass the numeric parse-error path and reach implementation-defined float-to-int conversion or later domain validation, producing misleading errors and inconsistent behavior across form surfaces.

Acceptance: centralize or consistently harden decimal-to-micros/basis-point parsing with math.IsNaN/math.IsInf checks before arithmetic, keep existing finite validation behavior, and add focused tests for NaN, +Inf, -Inf, ordinary negative, overflow, and valid decimal inputs across budget, resource usage, anomaly/payment reuse, and payment amount parsing.

title: Unify AnyCompany seed data source for migrations and reset templates
description:
Fresh-eyes quality review found the AnyCompany Retail seed organization is maintained in two independent places:
- Initial workspace migrations insert OUs/accounts in `internal/persistence/migrations/0016_anycompany_retail_organization.sql` and account tags in `0021_account_tags_seed_metadata.sql`.
- Reset/template code repeats the same organization, OUs, accounts, and account tags in `internal/persistence/organization_templates.go`.

Maintenance risk:
- A future seed-account, OU, or tag change can be applied to fresh workspaces but not reset workspaces, or vice versa.
- Scenario assumptions can drift depending on whether data came from initial migration or template reset.
- Reviewers must compare long SQL and Go literals manually.

Acceptance criteria:
- Establish one authoritative source for AnyCompany seed data, or add a generated/fixture consistency test that compares migration-created data with `anyCompanyRetailTemplateSeed()`.
- Cover organization units, accounts, lifecycle baseline fields, and account tags.
- Document the expected workflow for changing seed data.
- Existing organization, scenario reset, and migration tests pass.

title: Make payment lifecycle timestamp defaults explicit
description:
Fresh-eyes follow-up from bd-1y1m. Evidence: internal/persistence/payment_lifecycle.go:375-382 normalizes a blank PaymentLifecycleTransitionRequest.OccurredAt with time.Now().UTC().Format(time.RFC3339Nano). This keeps payment lifecycle repository behavior nondeterministic when callers omit OccurredAt, while app and scenario flows should prefer simulator-clock times.

Acceptance criteria:
- Replace the blank OccurredAt wall-clock fallback with an explicit required timestamp or an injected/simulator-clock boundary.
- Update app/scenario callers to pass deterministic simulator times where needed.
- Add tests proving blank OccurredAt behavior for payment lifecycle transitions.
- Preserve existing YYYY-MM-DD and RFC3339 parsing behavior for explicit values.

title: Use the AnyCompany management account as the default billing payer
description:
Fresh quality review found that the resource lab uses defaultAccountID 111122223333 both as the default usage account and as DefaultPayerAccountID. The seeded AnyCompany Retail organization marks 111122223333 as the Storefront Prod member account whose payer_account_id is 999988887777. As a result, the default billing pipeline, daily metering, clock-advance metering, month-end close, and Bills page workflows teach a member-account payer instead of consolidated management-account billing.

## Steps to Reproduce
1. Open a fresh workspace with the AnyCompany Retail fixture.
2. Visit the Resources page and inspect the payer_account_id values prefilled for billing pipeline, daily metering, and month-end close.
3. Observe they default to 111122223333 even though accounts.payer_account_id for that member account is 999988887777.
4. Run the default workflow and observe generated bill line items and closes use the member account as payer unless manually changed.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Default payer values in resource and bills workflows resolve from the seeded organization management account, or from the selected usage account payer relationship.
- Clock-advance daily metering uses the same resolved payer instead of a hard-coded member account.

title: Enforce billing visibility on payments workflow
description: Verified quality defect: billingvisibility.Policy denies ViewPayments to member-account viewers, but /payments and /payments/action do not resolve or enforce that policy. A member viewer can open payment-state data and a crafted POST can mutate invoice/payment-method state. title: Align docs on implemented exports assessment and scoped reconciliation

README.md lists implemented Exports UI, Query Lab examples, Payments UI, and learner progress or assessment feedback, but MIND_MAP.md node 8 still calls exports/payment-lab/assessment future work. title: Include viewer fields in export reconciliation filter state
description: Fresh-eyes review finding. internal/app/export_handlers.go parses viewer_role and viewer_account_id for /exports/reconciliation and applies them through exportPolicyFromValues, but exportReconciliationFilterFromRequest excludes those fields from HasFilters. The template only shows Clear when HasFilters is true, and handleCURReconciliation only attempts a report when HasFilters is true. title: Decompose largest app handlers into workflow-layer files
description:
Fresh-eyes quality review found several app handler files have accumulated many responsibilities and very high line counts:
- `internal/app/cost_explorer_handlers.go`: 2,173 lines, including handlers, builder parsing, saved-report actions, CSV export, line-item views, and SVG chart construction.
- `internal/app/resource_handlers.go`: 2,003 lines, including handlers, filters, form parsing, presets, table/view-model construction, and shared formatting helpers.
- `internal/app/export_handlers.go`: 1,822 lines, including direct downloads, stored export generation/regeneration, visibility scoping, filenames, reconciliation views, and templates.
- `internal/app/bill_handlers.go`: 1,620 lines, including bills page, invoices, CSV/PDF paths, visibility checks, read-model views, and templates.

Maintenance risk:
- Small workflow changes require scanning unrelated request parsing, view, export, and template code.
- New code has no obvious destination, so the largest files keep growing.
- Focused tests are harder to map to implementation modules.

Acceptance criteria:
- Split each targeted handler by stable responsibility, for example routes/handlers, form parsing, view-model mapping, exports/downloads, charts, and template definitions.
- Keep package boundaries and user-visible behavior unchanged.
- Prefer mechanical moves first, with focused follow-up tests only where behavior is touched.
- `go test ./internal/app` passes after each workflow split.

title: Keep past-due bill state during partial payment
description:
Fresh review found internal/persistence/payment_lifecycle.go updateBillStateForPaymentStatus maps every non-succeeded/non-past_due payment status back to bill_state='issued'. A past_due invoice that receives a partial payment transitions past_due -> partially_paid through ApplyPayment, so the bill row is reset to issued even though amount_due_micros remains greater than zero and the due date has passed. This can hide the bill from past-due summaries/cards until the learner marks it past due again.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Partial payments against past-due obligations preserve past-due bill visibility or expose an explicit partial-past-due state without reverting to issued.
- Bills and Payments UI state cards remain consistent after past_due -> partially_paid.
- Focused persistence and app/UI coverage exercises mark past due -> collect partial -> remaining balance stays past-due visible.

title: Split Cost Category repository storage from evaluation
description:
Fresh-eyes review: `internal/persistence/cost_categories.go` is 1,588 lines and the `CostCategoryRepository` file mixes multiple layers. It contains category CRUD starting around line 220, rule CRUD and conditions, preview construction at line 529, assignment refresh and summary refresh coupling around lines 642-930, SQL scanning and request normalization/validation around lines 1067-1346, and the recursive `costCategoryPreviewEvaluator` around lines 1347-1485.

Maintenance risk:
- Storage, validation, preview read models, assignment persistence, Cost Explorer summary refresh, and recursive rule evaluation are coupled in one file.
- Rule-evaluator changes can accidentally affect repository SQL/scanning or assignment refresh behavior.
- Split-charge refresh is triggered from assignment refresh, creating cross-domain behavior that is easy to miss during Cost Category edits.

Acceptance criteria:
- Move rule evaluation/preview logic into a focused evaluator unit with tests that cover ordering, shadowing, and reference cycles.
- Separate assignment refresh/persistence from category/rule CRUD where practical, preserving public repository methods.
- Keep open-period/finalized-period behavior unchanged; run `go test ./internal/persistence`.

title: Resolve label-scoped work ordering conflict in agent docs
description:

AGENTS.md line 41 says to check `bv --robot-next` at session start, while MIND_MAP.md node [29] says label-scoped quality work should select from `br ready --label quality` before `bv --robot-next`. title: Make the race test gate usable for app HTTP tests
description:
Fresh-eyes review finding.

Evidence:
- go test ./... passes.
- go test -race ./... fails in internal/app because several httptest clients time out after 1 second while opening or starting workspaces under race instrumentation. Observed failing tests: TestOrganizationHierarchyEditorFeatureWorksInFreshWorkspace, TestLocalServerSmokeFlowCreatesWorkspaceAndServesDashboard, TestServerRenderedUIShellFeatureWorksInFreshWorkspace, TestWorkspaceUIStartsFreshExperienceAndPersistsGeneratedPath, and TestWorkspaceUICreatesWorkspaceAndPersistsLastPath.
- Representative error: POST /workspaces/open error = context deadline exceeded (Client.Timeout exceeded while awaiting headers).

Impact:
The project cannot use the race detector as a reliable quality gate for app-level concurrency work. The failures look like brittle test timeouts around workspace open/migrations rather than a demonstrated product race.

Acceptance notes:
- Replace 1s HTTP client timeouts in app smoke/workspace tests with a race-safe timeout or helper that scales under -race.
- Keep ordinary go test ./... runtime reasonable.
- Verify go test -race ./internal/app and, ideally, go test -race ./... complete without these deadline failures.

title: Avoid direct export CSV body generation for HEAD requests
description: Follow-up from bd-wiug. The direct /exports/cur.csv and /exports/focus.csv handlers accept HEAD, but they still call WriteCSVExport/WriteFOCUSCSVExport into a bytes.Buffer before skipping the response write. Acceptance: those direct export CSV endpoints preserve GET download headers for HEAD while returning before CSV body rendering or body writes, with recorder-based HEAD tests alongside existing GET export coverage.

title: Replace string-matched domain error handling with sentinel errors
description:
Fresh-eyes quality review found production control flow that classifies errors by message text:
- `internal/scenario/runner.go:isClosedPeriodPricingFailure` checks `strings.Contains(err.Error(), "billing period is closed for payer")`.
- `Runner.createCostCategory` treats any error containing `"not found"` as the expected missing-category case.
- `internal/scenario/checks.go:evaluateCostCategoryRuleCreated` checks for `"no rows"` in a lowercased error string.

Maintenance risk:
- Rewording a persistence error can silently change control flow.
- Localization or wrapping changes can break scenario rerun and feedback behavior.
- Tests may overfit user-facing error text rather than stable error categories.

Acceptance criteria:
- Define sentinel/domain errors for closed-period mutation, missing cost category, missing saved report/entity, and other currently string-matched cases.
- Wrap errors with `%w` and use `errors.Is`/`errors.As` in production branches.
- Keep learner-facing messages unchanged where they are part of current UX.
- Add focused tests proving the branches work through wrapped errors and do not depend on exact message text.

title: Extract large app page templates from handler files

title: Clarify unused Savings Plan commitment in amortized Cost Explorer totals
description:
Fresh-eyes review found amortized_cost behaves like allocated covered-usage cost, not full amortized cost.

Evidence:
- internal/persistence/cost_explorer.go costExplorerAmortizedCostExpression returns 0 for generated Savings Plan fee line items.
- Usage rows only receive allocations from negation source rows; fee source rows have no source_bill_line_item_id per internal/persistence/migrations/0039_savings_plans.sql, so partially or fully unused commitment is not included anywhere.
- internal/persistence/cost_explorer_test.go TestCostExplorerRepositoryReportsDiscountCostMetrics codifies net 390000 vs amortized 216320, dropping unused commitment.

Impact: a user selecting Amortized Cost may see totals lower than actual amortized spend when a plan is underused. README.md currently uses wording around amortized source-allocation, so the product contract needs to be explicit.

Acceptance:
- Decide whether amortized_cost must include unused commitment or be labeled/explained as source allocation.
- Add tests for partially unused and fully unused Savings Plans.
- If keeping source-allocation semantics, make UI/API naming clear enough that totals are not mistaken for full amortized cost.

title: Preserve export viewer context on POST errors
description:
Export generation and regeneration POST handlers validate viewer_role/viewer_account_id from the submitted form, but render error pages through renderExports, which rebuilds page state from r.URL.Query(). When the POST body carries a member/finance viewer and validation or authorization fails, the response drops the submitted simulated viewer context from filters, action links, and forms.

Acceptance criteria:
