- Existing GET export rendering behavior is unchanged.
- Focused app test covers a failing export POST path with member viewer fields.

title: Decompose export workflow helpers by responsibility
description:
Fresh-eyes review: after the previous export split, `internal/app/export_workflows.go` is still 836 lines and owns several unrelated responsibilities. It parses CUR/reconciliation requests (lines 17-60), generates and writes stored CUR/FOCUS exports (lines 63-146), resolves/scopes viewer policies (lines 148-303), builds export list/filter/table view state (lines 324-459), reconstructs requests from stored metadata (lines 460-492), formats display values and filenames (roughly 494 onward), and builds reconciliation view helpers later in the same file.

Maintenance risk:
- Authorization/scope bugs, filename changes, stored-export regeneration, and UI table changes all share one helper file.
- FOCUS and CUR behavior can drift because parallel generation/regeneration logic is adjacent but not factored by export kind.
- New export types would need changes across parsing, scoping, persistence, filenames, rows, and templates without clear extension points.

Acceptance criteria:
- Split export helpers into focused units such as request parsing, visibility/scoping, persisted generation/regeneration, filename/metadata, and view/table mapping.
- Keep route behavior and existing filenames/checksum semantics unchanged.
- Add focused coverage for any moved authorization or generation behavior; `go test ./internal/app` passes.

title: Add Cost Explorer variance investigation scenario
description:
## Context
Current packaged scenarios seed Cost Explorer data, but they do not force a learner through the common real-world FinOps activity of explaining why one month is higher than another. Existing labs either resume into a prepared Cost Explorer view or focus on tags, allocation, payments, budgets, Savings Plans, or a single untagged spike.

## User Story
As a learner acting as a finance or platform analyst, I want to investigate a month-over-month cost increase from a vague prompt, use Cost Explorer to isolate the driver, save evidence, and explain the owning account/team so I can practice a realistic billing review workflow.

## Proposed Scenario
Create a packaged scenario tentatively named "Cost Explorer variance investigation".

The lab should seed at least two comparable billing periods in AnyCompany Retail, with one current period materially higher than the baseline. The increase should require iterative analysis rather than being obvious from a single total. A strong shape would be:
- baseline month with normal Storefront, Payments, Analytics, and Shared Networking usage
- current month with total spend up roughly 30-50 percent
- one primary driver such as Storefront EC2 scale-out, NAT/data-transfer growth, or a new RDS workload
- at least one plausible distractor such as steady S3 growth, support charges, or unrelated Payments usage
- useful tags and account metadata so the learner can identify owner/team, not only service

## Expected Learner Workflow
The learner should be able to:
1. Launch the scenario from `/scenarios` with curated phase, objective, estimate, and resume link.
2. Start from a broad Cost Explorer view comparing or reviewing monthly spend, not from a fully solved drilldown.
3. Change Cost Explorer grouping across service, linked account, usage type, and tag or Cost Category to find the top mover.
4. Drill into source bill line items for the suspected driver.
5. Save or update a Cost Explorer report that captures the explanation.
6. Optionally export CSV evidence or link to line-item drilldown evidence if existing UI supports it cleanly.
7. Read scenario feedback explaining what changed, which data supports it, and how this maps to real AWS billing variance review.

## Acceptance Criteria
- A new packaged scenario definition is added under `internal/scenario/seeds` and registered in the seed catalog.
- `/scenarios` lists the lab with curated metadata, likely Phase 2, an estimated duration around 10 minutes, and a Cost Explorer resume path.
- Scenario execution deterministically creates baseline and current period line items suitable for month-over-month analysis in a fresh workspace.
- Cost Explorer can reveal the variance through existing controls: date range, monthly granularity, groupings, filters, saved report, drilldown, and CSV export where applicable.
- Scenario checks validate at least the saved report and the identified top cost driver. If existing check types are insufficient, add the smallest reusable check extension needed for variance investigation.
- Learner feedback names the changed state, evidence source tables, and AWS billing concept mapping for the variance investigation path.
- Existing scenarios continue to run and catalog parity tests cover the new seed.
- README.md and MIND_MAP.md are updated to mention the new lab and its Cost Explorer learning objective.

## Suggested Tests
- Scenario parser/seed catalog parity coverage includes the new seed key.
- Scenario runner test proves the seed launches successfully and creates both baseline and current-period bill data.
- Scenario check test proves the expected top driver and saved report pass after launch.
- App scenario page test verifies the title, objective, duration, launch form, and resume link.
- Focused Cost Explorer test or existing query coverage proves the seeded data produces the intended top mover when grouped by service/account/usage type.

## Out of Scope
- Building a full Cost Explorer comparison UI if the current report builder cannot compare periods directly; the scenario can use monthly grouped results and seeded saved report workflow first.
- Real AWS Cost Anomaly Detection behavior, notifications, or subscriptions.
- New external SQL/DuckDB dependency.
- Credits, taxes, or real pricing fidelity beyond current synthetic catalog boundaries.

## Notes
Prefer cucumber-style scenario coverage if a higher-level scenario test harness is already available; otherwise keep Go tests focused around the scenario runner, Cost Explorer query behavior, and `/scenarios` UI.

title: Fix fresh workspace names to include real subsecond entropy
description: Fresh-eyes review found workspaceSession.NewFreshWorkspacePath builds names with time.Now().UTC().Format("20060102-150405-000000000") in internal/app/workspace.go. In Go, fractional seconds are only formatted after a decimal point, so the trailing 000000000 is literal text rather than nanoseconds. Multiple starts in the same second fall back to -01/-02 suffix probing, which works but is not the intended unique timestamp and is not covered by tests. Acceptance: use a timestamp format or UnixNano-derived suffix that contains real subsecond entropy, add a focused test that would fail with the literal-zero layout, and keep the existing active-workspace/state-path parent selection behavior.

title: Split scenario action implementation by action family
description:
Fresh-eyes review finding. Evidence: internal/scenario/runner.go is 1,252 lines and still owns action-family implementation methods such as createSavingsPlan, createBudget, createPaymentMethod, createCostCategory*, createAccount, createResource, addUsage, and generateUsage. internal/scenario/event_actions.go is 852 lines and mixes the action registry, normalization, validation, preflight adapters, and apply wrappers.

Acceptance: split scenario action implementation by domain/action family so the registry stays declarative, action-local payload/validation/apply code lives near each other, and TestScenarioEventActionRegistryCoversSupportedActions plus focused runner/parser tests continue to pass.

title: Preserve Cost Explorer owner scope on New Report
description: The Cost Explorer page correctly scopes saved-report shelves and query visibility by owner_account_id + owner_role, but the toolbar New Report action is hardcoded as href=/cost-explorer in internal/app/cost_explorer_handlers.go. When a learner is operating as a member-account or finance owner, clicking New Report drops the current owner fields and reloads the default management-account owner, which changes the visible data/saved-report shelf instead of merely clearing the report definition. Acceptance: build the New Report href from the current builder owner fields, clearing saved_report_id/report filters while preserving owner_account_id and owner_role; add a browser-facing test that a member owner context keeps name=owner_account_id value and owner_role after using the new-report path.

title: Render negative Cost Explorer metric values in charts
description:
Fresh-eyes review found Cost Explorer charting drops negative selected metric values.

Evidence:
- internal/app/cost_explorer_charts.go: costExplorerChartMaxValue and costExplorerChartY clamp values below 0 to 0; bar rendering also clamps negative heights to 0.
- internal/persistence/cost_explorer.go: costExplorerNetCostExpression intentionally makes Credit and Refund rows negative, so a negative net-cost bucket or group can render as zero/no bar.

Impact: net-cost credits/refunds can disappear from line, bar, and stacked charts, teaching users the value is zero.

Acceptance:
- Add focused chart tests with negative net-cost buckets/groups.
- Render a visible negative axis/baseline or other explicit negative-value treatment instead of clamping to zero.
- Keep positive-only charts unchanged.

title: Align robot-next tracker commands with br workflow guidance
description:
Fresh quality review found that AGENTS.md and MIND_MAP.md tell agents to use br in this workspace, but bv --robot-next currently emits bd command strings such as bd update and bd show. That creates a process trap at session start: agents following the robot-next JSON literally can violate the local br-only workflow or hit the legacy CLI behavior that MIND_MAP.md already warns about.

## Steps to Reproduce
1. Run bv --robot-next from the repository root.
2. Observe claim_command and show_command use bd.
3. Compare AGENTS.md, which says the project uses br and lists br ready, br show, br update, br close, and br sync.
4. Compare MIND_MAP.md node [14], which says to use br in this workspace.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Session-start guidance is internally consistent: robot-next output emits br commands, or AGENTS.md documents the exact conversion from bd-form robot output to br commands.
- MIND_MAP.md notes are updated if the expected tracker command source changes.
- A lightweight smoke check or documented verification prevents future guidance from drifting back to contradictory bd/br commands.

title: Avoid CUR export filename collisions across request variants
description: Fresh-eyes review finding. CUR CSV filenames only include period start, period end, and payer account, but the export request and stored metadata also vary by usage_account_id, line_item_status, and limit. ExportFileRepository.Write upserts by filename, so generating two variants for the same payer-period overwrites the earlier file and metadata while the exports page can only show one variant. Acceptance criteria: make stored filenames or metadata identity include every request dimension that changes content, preserve distinct all-account/account/status/limit variants through direct download and regeneration, and add coverage that generates at least two variants for the same payer-period and sees distinct metadata rows/files/bodies.

Acceptance criteria: decide whether the exports inventory means most recently changed or first created, align the SQL and UI copy to that behavior, and add coverage showing a regenerated older export appears in the expected order.

title: Reconcile export and query-lab completion state across docs and tracker

However `br ready` still returns the open export/query-lab epic `bd-hz2`, and `br show bd-hz2.2` plus `br show bd-hz2.3` show open feature wrappers even though their named children are closed. title: Make MIND_MAP current-work pointers less stale-prone
description:
Fresh-eyes cohesion finding. MIND_MAP.md node [12] says live backlog state wins, but it still contains a very long closed-issue ledger under Current Work Pointers. That ledger already omits newer closed quality issues visible in `br list --all --label quality`, such as `bd-xit5`, `bd-38n4`, `bd-1g2b`, and `bd-3ers`, while node [30] says future updates should avoid repeating issue IDs and prefer durable patterns. The result is a node that looks authoritative but is hard to keep current.

Acceptance criteria:
- Replace or shorten the stale closed-issue ledger in node [12] so live `br` commands remain the source of truth.
- Keep any retained examples explicitly dated or framed as historical examples, not a complete current list.
- Align node [12] with node [30] by preserving durable navigation pointers and moving reusable lessons into existing topical nodes instead of expanding the ledger.

title: Extract shared cost allocation tag coverage aggregation
description:
Fresh-eyes quality review finding.

Evidence:
- internal/persistence/cost_allocation_tags.go ListCoverage builds coverage accumulators, key/account/service rows, case-mismatch state, and sorting inline.
- internal/persistence/cost_explorer_summaries.go costExplorerSummaryCoverageRows duplicates the same accumulator construction and nested key/item loop.

Problem:
The tag manager and Cost Explorer summary refresh can drift when coverage dimensions, case-mismatch handling, activation visibility, or sorting rules change. This is pure domain logic duplication, not just file size.

Suggested outcome:
Extract one shared pure helper for building CostAllocationTagCoverageRow values from discovered keys and coverage line items. Use it from both ListCoverage and Cost Explorer summary refresh, keeping query-specific loading separate. Add focused parity coverage so a future dimension or case-mismatch change only needs one implementation update.

title: Shrink scenario event payload contracts around action-specific fields
description:
Fresh-eyes quality review finding and follow-up to closed bd-1wpm.

Evidence:
- bd-1wpm completed the first scenario action cleanup by moving dispatch into internal/scenario/event_actions.go action specs.
- internal/scenario/parser.go still defines Event as a single broad struct containing scheduling, account, resource, usage, billing, Cost Category, split-charge, payment, Savings Plan, budget, and saved-report fields.
- internal/scenario/event_actions.go now has a useful action registry, but normalize/validate/preflight/apply hooks all still receive the same wide Event value.

Problem:
The registry simplified action dispatch, but the data contract is still effectively one mega-payload. That leaves action-specific requirements spread across normalization and validation helpers, makes unsupported field combinations easy to miss, and makes new scenario actions add more optional fields to an already broad struct.

Suggested outcome:
Introduce action-specific payload structs or smaller field groups behind the existing JSON/YAML surface, then have action specs normalize/validate/apply against those narrower contracts. Preserve existing scenario files and learner-facing validation messages. Cover at least one migrated action family first, then repeat incrementally if the shape proves cleaner.

title: Add HEAD coverage for stored export downloads
description:
Fresh-eyes test review found strong HEAD coverage for direct CSV/PDF downloads, including `TestDirectCSVExportHEADSkipsCSVRendering` in `internal/app/exports_test.go` and invoice/cost-explorer download helpers. Stored export file downloads in `handleExportFileDownload` (`internal/app/export_handlers.go`) have extensive GET, visibility, checksum, and regeneration assertions in `TestCURCSVExportDownloadIncludesBillMetadata`, but no direct HEAD assertion for `/exports/files/{filename}`.

Add focused tests after generating stored CUR and FOCUS metadata exports that call HEAD on the stored download URLs. Assert status 200, no body bytes via `httptest.ResponseRecorder`, and parity for Content-Type, Content-Disposition, Content-Length, X-Simulator-Export-Filename, and X-Simulator-Export-Checksum. Include a member-scoped stored export case so visibility metadata continues to gate HEAD the same way as GET.

title: Avoid generating download bodies for HEAD requests
description: Several downloadable routes accept HEAD but still build and write the full response body in the handler: /cost-explorer/results.csv writes body unconditionally, and invoice CSV/PDF handlers do the same. The export download handlers already skip writes for HEAD. Acceptance: Cost Explorer CSV and invoice CSV/PDF endpoints return the same headers for HEAD without generating or writing body bytes, and tests cover HEAD behavior alongside existing GET download tests.

title: Prevent cross-scope CUR export filename collisions
description: Stored CUR CSV filenames include period, payer, usage account, status, and limit, but not the billing visibility scope. A member-scoped export and a management-scoped usage-account export can therefore share one filename. If the broader export overwrites the stored file, a member download can receive payer document metadata such as source_bill_id. Add regression coverage and make stored export variants scope-distinct.

title: Guard workspace database swaps against in-flight requests
description:
Fresh-eyes review finding.

Evidence:
- internal/app/workspace.go:108-137: workspaceSession.Open saves and swaps s.db under the mutex, releases the mutex, then closes oldDB.
- internal/app/workspace.go:143-146: workspaceSession.DB returns the raw *sql.DB after releasing the mutex.
- internal/app/workspace_handlers.go routes build handlers from workspace.DB() per request, so a request can capture oldDB just before /workspaces/open, /workspaces/start, /scenarios/reset, or /scenarios/clone closes it.

Impact:
Concurrent browser activity, partial-refresh requests, exports, or long-running report requests can fail mid-handler with sql: database is closed during workspace swaps. Reset also temporarily sets s.db nil while requests may already hold the old handle.

Acceptance notes:
- Introduce a request-scoped DB lease/refcount or otherwise serialize workspace swaps with active requests.
- Workspace open/reset/clone should wait for active handlers or make old handles safe until handlers finish.
- Add a focused test that holds a workspace-backed request while a workspace swap happens and proves neither request sees a closed DB error.

title: Split or reject usage windows that cross billing periods
description:
Fresh quality review found billLineItemFromMeteringRecord assigns an entire usage window to the month containing usage_start_time. The existing boundary test expects 2026-02-28T22:00Z through 2026-03-01T02:00Z to stay wholly in February. That will distort monthly bills, Cost Explorer summaries, invoices, and reconciliation unless cross-period usage is split or rejected before pricing.

## Steps to Reproduce
1. Create an active EC2 resource.
2. Record usage from 2026-02-28T22:00:00Z through 2026-03-01T02:00:00Z with 4 instance hours.
3. Generate metering records and bill line items.
4. Observe a single February billing-period line item for all 4 hours, including the March hours.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Usage that spans a billing period boundary is split into period-contained metering or line-item rows with proportional quantities, or rejected upstream with a clear validation error.
- Boundary tests prove March usage is not silently billed to February.
- Month-end close, summaries, invoices, and exports consume only period-contained billable rows.

title: Use actual billing-period days in resource-lab cost estimates
description:
Fresh quality review found resourceLabHandler.usageEventView prices recent usage with BillingPeriodDays hard-coded to 30. The persisted bill-line-item path uses the actual month length, so resource-lab estimates for GB-day to GB-month storage are wrong in February, leap-year February, March, and any 31-day month.

## Steps to Reproduce
1. Create a storage resource and record one 100 GBDay usage event on 2026-02-01.
2. View the Resources lab Recent Usage estimate.
3. Generate the corresponding bill line item.
4. Compare the UI estimate against persisted pricing; the UI used 30 days while the line-item path used 28 days.

## Acceptance Criteria
- UI estimates derive billing-period days from the usage start date using the same logic as bill line items.
- Tests cover February and 31-day storage estimates matching persisted pricing.
- Any shared billing-period helper avoids duplicating month-length logic between UI and persistence.

title: Generate Support charges from eligible monthly spend
description:
Fresh quality review found the seeded catalog includes AWSSupport with formula eligible_monthly_cost * rate_with_minimum, but the current pricing path only prices individual metering records. There is no period-level job that computes eligible monthly charges, applies the support percentage/minimum rule, and emits a support bill line item. This can mislead later Bills and Cost Explorer work because the catalog advertises Support while the engine cannot derive it correctly.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Support pricing is generated from finalized or estimated eligible monthly spend, not from ad hoc user usage events.
- The synthetic percentage and minimum rule is implemented deliberately and documented in tests.
- Ineligible line item types are excluded from the eligible-spend base.
- Reconciliation tests prove support totals trace back to eligible source line items.

title: Reject usage outside resource lifecycle windows
description:
Fresh quality review found ResourceUsageRepository.RecordUsageEvent only checks service match and the usage window shape. It does not reject usage for planned resources, usage before started_at, usage after stopped_at or deleted_at, or generated patterns for resources that are not active during the requested dates. The UI exposes planned, stopped, and deleted resource states, so impossible charges can enter metering and line items.

## Steps to Reproduce
1. Create a resource with status planned and no started_at.
2. Record usage for that resource with any valid usage window.
3. Observe the usage event is accepted and can later be metered and priced.
4. Repeat with a stopped resource and a usage window after stopped_at; observe the same acceptance.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Repository validation enforces resource lifecycle windows for manual and generated usage.
- Tests cover planned resources, before-start windows, after-stop windows, and after-delete windows.
- Any allowed stopped-resource charges are explicitly bounded through the stop timestamp.

title: Report created versus reused generated usage events
description:
Fresh quality review found GenerateUsage uses deterministic IDs and ON CONFLICT(id) DO NOTHING, then returns the existing rows. That makes reruns idempotent at the database level, but the result only exposes Events and the UI says Generated N usage events even when a rerun inserted zero new rows.

## Steps to Reproduce
1. Create an S3 resource.
2. Generate the requests pattern for 3 days.
3. Generate the same pattern again for the same resource and date range.
4. Observe the database row count is unchanged, but the result and UI still report the full generated event count.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Generation results expose created and reused counts, or equivalent status.
- The resource-lab flash distinguishes no-op reruns from new inserts.
- Tests cover repeated generation reporting without increasing usage_events.

title: Prevent payer mismatches from closing bills with stranded line items
description:
Fresh quality review found that line items are permanently keyed to the payer used when pricing runs. If daily metering or the manual billing pipeline prices usage with one payer, a later month-end close for a different payer sees the metering records as already priced, creates no replacement line items for the requested payer, and can issue a zero or incomplete bill while the original estimated line items remain open under the other payer. This is especially risky now that AnyCompany member accounts have a separate management payer.

## Steps to Reproduce
1. Create usage for account 111122223333 in a fresh workspace.
2. Run daily metering or the billing pipeline with payer_account_id 111122223333 so estimated bill_line_items are created for the member payer.
3. Advance the simulator clock past the billing period and close the same period with payer_account_id 999988887777.
4. Observe the close succeeds for the management payer with zero or missing finalized usage, because the metering records were already consumed by member-payer line items.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Month-end close refuses payer-mismatched prepriced line items with an actionable error, or reassigns/reprices them through an explicit consolidated-billing rule.
- Tests cover a member-payer daily metering run followed by a management-payer close.
- Bills, reconciliation, and invoice totals cannot silently omit usage because the payer chosen during earlier estimated pricing differs from the close payer.
